mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and income expansion, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current payroll level 132,697k indicates sustained employment growth trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 1.5% in a tightening cycle, equity valuations compress as the risk-free rate increases and discount rates rise, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.1% indicates substantial room for tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) signals broad-based demand expansion across residential and consumer sectors, indicating sustained economic momentum that drives equity valuations higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 699k and RSAFS at 407,805M show solid demand conditions
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 58% of months where both HOUST and RSAFS show month-over-month growth, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining more than 1.5% year-over-year signals supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction, which reduces corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.9054 (2017=100) reflects current production levels
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 52% of instances where INDPRO YoY decline > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.4 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 8.3% provides baseline for deterioration measurement
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 48% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary policy tightening pressure, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 227.505 and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% show current inflation-rate environment
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized signals weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations are repriced.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,429.9B (chained 2017 dollars) as of 5 months prior to decision date
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with payroll expansion (PAYEMS) signals synchronized supply and labor capacity growth, indicating the economy can sustain higher output without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.9054 and PAYEMS at 132,697k show current supply-labor conditions
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 56% of months where both INDPRO and PAYEMS show month-over-month growth, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.
